Paf公司全面采用ChatGPT企业版，提升开发效率和培训质量。



摘要： Paf公司已在其整个组织中采用ChatGPT企业版，工程师每天使用定制的GPT来加速日常开发任务。同时，Paf将ChatGPT企业版整合到grit:lab编码学院中，通过AI增强的系统架构思维培养下一代软件开发者。此外，70%的Paf员工在包括财务、人力资源、市场营销和客户支持等业务团队中积极使用ChatGPT企业版。

Stack Overflow and OpenAI today announced a new API partnership that will empower developers with the collective strengths of the world’s leading knowledge platform for highly technical content with the world’s most popular LLM models for AI development.

item.title: 大规模视频生成模型训练研究
item.content:encoded:
视频生成模型通过扩展实现物理世界模拟。



摘要： 本文探索了在视频数据上进行大规模生成模型训练的方法，特别针对不同持续时间、分辨率和画面比例的视频和图像进行联合训练。研究采用基于时空块的Transformer架构，对视频和图像的潜在表示进行处理。最大的模型Sora能够生成高质量的一分钟视频，实验结果表明，扩展视频生成模型是构建物理世界通用模拟器的有前景方向。

item.content:encoded: We’re testing the ability for ChatGPT to remember things you discuss to make future chats more helpful. You’re in control of ChatGPT’s memory.

item.title: 评估大语言模型在生物威胁创建中的风险
item.content:encoded:
GPT-4对生物威胁创建的提升有限，需持续研究。



摘要： 本文提出了一种评估大语言模型（LLM）可能被用于创建生物威胁的风险蓝图。通过让生物专家和学生参与测试，发现GPT-4在提高生物威胁创建准确性方面仅有轻微提升。尽管这一提升并不显著，但该研究为后续的深入探讨和社区讨论提供了基础。

item.content:encoded:
GPT-4提升内容审核效率与一致性。



摘要： 文章介绍了GPT-4在内容政策制定和审核决策中的应用，通过自动化标签系统提高审核一致性，加快政策优化的反馈循环，并减少人工审核人员的参与。该技术有助于提升内容管理的效率和准确性，同时降低对人力的依赖。

item.title: AI发展助力本地新闻创新

item.title: Frontier AI regulation: Managing emerging risks to public safety
item.content:encoded: Frontier AI regulation: Managing emerging risks to public safety

item.title: Insights from global conversations
item.content:encoded: We are sharing what we learned from our conversations across 22 countries, and how we will be incorporating those insights moving forward.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI London
item.content:encoded: We are excited to announce OpenAI’s first international expansion with a new office in London, United Kingdom.

item.title: Function calling and other API updates
item.content:encoded: We’re announcing updates including more steerable API models, function calling capabilities, longer context, and lower prices.

item.title: OpenAI Cybersecurity Grant Program
item.content:encoded: Our goal is to facilitate the development of AI-powered cybersecurity capabilities for defenders through grants and other support.

item.title: Improving mathematical reasoning with process supervision
item.content:encoded: We've trained a model to achieve a new state-of-the-art in mathematical problem solving by rewarding each correct step of reasoning (“process supervision”) instead of simply rewarding the correct final answer (“outcome supervision”). In addition to boosting performance relative to outcome supervision, process supervision also has an important alignment benefit: it directly trains the model to produce a chain-of-thought that is endorsed by humans.

item.title: Democratic inputs to AI
item.content:encoded: Our nonprofit organization, OpenAI, Inc., is launching a program to award ten $100,000 grants to fund experiments in setting up a democratic process for deciding what rules AI systems should follow, within the bounds defined by the law.

item.title: Governance of superintelligence
item.content:encoded: Now is a good time to start thinking about the governance of superintelligence—future AI systems dramatically more capable than even AGI.

item.title: Introducing the ChatGPT app for iOS
item.content:encoded: The ChatGPT app syncs your conversations, supports voice input, and brings our latest model improvements to your fingertips.

item.title: Language models can explain neurons in language models
item.content:encoded: We use GPT-4 to automatically write explanations for the behavior of neurons in large language models and to score those explanations. We release a dataset of these (imperfect) explanations and scores for every neuron in GPT-2.

item.title: New ways to manage your data in ChatGPT
item.content:encoded: ChatGPT users can now turn off chat history, allowing you to choose which conversations can be used to train our models.

item.title: Announcing OpenAI’s Bug Bounty Program
item.content:encoded: This initiative is essential to our commitment to develop safe and advanced AI. As we create technology and services that are secure, reliable, and trustworthy, we need your help.

item.title: Our approach to AI safety
item.content:encoded: Ensuring that AI systems are built, deployed, and used safely is critical to our mission.

item.title: March 20 ChatGPT outage: Here’s what happened
item.content:encoded: An update on our findings, the actions we’ve taken, and technical details of the bug.

item.title: ChatGPT plugins
item.content:encoded: We’ve implemented initial support for plugins in ChatGPT. Plugins are tools designed specifically for language models with safety as a core principle, and help ChatGPT access up-to-date information, run computations, or use third-party services.

item.title: GPTs are GPTs: An early look at the labor market impact potential of large language models
item.content:encoded: GPTs are GPTs: An early look at the labor market impact potential of large language models

item.title: GPT-4：OpenAI的最新深度学习里程碑
item.content:encoded:
GPT-4是OpenAI推出的大型多模态模型，表现接近人类水平。

item.title: 大型语言模型在信息战中的潜在滥用风险研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨了大语言模型被用于制造虚假信息的风险及应对框架。



摘要： OpenAI研究人员与乔治城大学安全与新兴技术研究中心及斯坦福互联网观察站合作，研究大型语言模型可能被滥用于制造虚假信息的问题。该合作包括2021年10月的研讨会，汇聚了30位研究者、机器学习专家和政策分析人员，并基于一年多的研究发布了一份联合报告。报告阐述了语言模型在信息环境中可能带来的威胁，并提出了分析潜在缓解措施的框架。

item.title: ChatGPT：一种对话式人工智能模型
item.content:encoded:
ChatGPT是一种能够进行对话的AI模型，具备回答问题、承认错误和拒绝不当请求的能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了ChatGPT，这是一种能够以对话方式与用户互动的人工智能模型。通过对话格式，ChatGPT可以回答后续问题、承认自身错误、挑战不正确的前提，并拒绝不适当的请求。该模型展示了在自然语言处理和人机交互方面的先进能力，为用户提供更加灵活和智能的交流体验。

item.content:encoded:
改进AI系统从人类反馈学习，以辅助评估AI系统。



摘要： 文章介绍了如何提升AI系统从人类反馈中学习的能力，并利用这些能力来帮助人类评估AI。目标是构建一个与人类价值观充分对齐的AI系统，从而解决其他所有对齐问题。该研究强调了AI系统在学习和评估过程中的重要性，并提出了通过人类反馈优化AI性能的方法。

item.title: Reducing bias and improving safety in DALL·E 2
item.content:encoded: Today, we are implementing a new technique so that DALL·E generates images of people that more accurately reflect the diversity of the world’s population.

item.title: DALL·E 2: Extending creativity
item.content:encoded: As part of our DALL·E 2 research preview, more than 3,000 artists from more than 118 countries have incorporated DALL·E into their creative workflows. The artists in our early access group have helped us discover new uses for DALL·E and have served as key voices as we’ve made decisions about DALL·E’s features.

item.title: DALL·E 2 pre-training mitigations
item.content:encoded: In order to share the magic of DALL·E 2 with a broad audience, we needed to reduce the risks associated with powerful image generation models. To this end, we put various guardrails in place to prevent generated images from violating our content policy.

item.title: Learning to play Minecraft with Video PreTraining
item.content:encoded: We trained a neural network to play Minecraft by Video PreTraining (VPT) on a massive unlabeled video dataset of human Minecraft play, while using only a small amount of labeled contractor data. With fine-tuning, our model can learn to craft diamond tools, a task that usually takes proficient humans over 20 minutes (24,000 actions). Our model uses the native human interface of keypresses and mouse movements, making it quite general, and represents a step towards general computer-using agents.

item.title: Evolution through large models
item.content:encoded: Evolution through large models

item.title: AI-written critiques help humans notice flaws
item.content:encoded: We trained “critique-writing” models to describe flaws in summaries. Human evaluators find flaws in summaries much more often when shown our model’s critiques. Larger models are better at self-critiquing, with scale improving critique-writing more than summary-writing. This shows promise for using AI systems to assist human supervision of AI systems on difficult tasks.

item.title: Techniques for training large neural networks
item.content:encoded: Large neural networks are at the core of many recent advances in AI, but training them is a difficult engineering and research challenge which requires orchestrating a cluster of GPUs to perform a single synchronized calculation.

item.title: Best practices for deploying language models
item.content:encoded: Cohere, OpenAI, and AI21 Labs have developed a preliminary set of best practices applicable to any organization developing or deploying large language models.

item.title: Teaching models to express their uncertainty in words
item.content:encoded: Teaching models to express their uncertainty in words

item.title: Powering next generation applications with OpenAI Codex
item.content:encoded: Codex is now powering 70 different applications across a variety of use cases through the OpenAI API.

item.title: DALL·E 2 research preview update
item.content:encoded: Early users have created over 3 million images to date and helped us improve our safety processes. We’re excited to begin adding up to 1,000 new users from our waitlist each week.

item.title: OpenAI leadership team update
item.content:encoded: We’re happy to announce several executive role changes that reflect our recent progress and will ensure continued momentum toward our next major milestones.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.

item.title: OpenAI and Malta partner to bring ChatGPT Plus to all citizens
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Malta partner to expand AI access, offering ChatGPT Plus and training to help citizens build practical AI skills and use AI responsibly.

item.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
item.content:encoded: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

item.title: How business operations teams use ChatGPT Work
item.content:encoded: See how business operations teams can use ChatGPT Work to create initiative briefs, strategy updates, leadership decision packets, progress updates, and more from real work inputs

item.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
item.content:encoded: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.

item.title: Measuring Goodhart’s law
item.content:encoded: Goodhart’s law famously says: “When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.” Although originally from economics, it’s something we have to grapple with at OpenAI when figuring out how to optimize objectives that are difficult or costly to measure.

item.title: New GPT-3 capabilities: Edit & insert
item.content:encoded: We’ve released new versions of GPT-3 and Codex which can edit or insert content into existing text, rather than just completing existing text.

item.content:encoded: We describe our latest thinking in the hope of helping other AI developers address safety and misuse of deployed models.

item.content:encoded:
神经定理证明器成功解决AMC12、AIME及IMO改编题。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于Lean的神经定理证明器，该系统能够解决多种具有挑战性的高中数学竞赛问题，包括AMC12和AIME竞赛题目，以及两道来自IMO的改编题。该模型展示了在自动定理证明领域的潜在应用价值，特别是在处理复杂数学推理任务方面的能力。

item.content:encoded:
新型系统数学解题准确率接近人类儿童水平。



摘要： 研究人员开发了一种新的系统，能够以接近人类儿童的水平解决小学数学问题。该系统在测试中的准确率几乎达到真实9-12岁儿童的60%，而其准确率是经过微调的GPT-3模型的两倍。这表明该系统在解决基础数学问题方面具有显著优势。

item.content:encoded:
Triton 1.0让无CUDA经验的研究者也能编写高效GPU代码。



摘要： Triton 1.0是一款开源的Python-like编程语言，旨在帮助没有CUDA经验的研究人员编写高效的GPU代码。该工具使得大多数情况下，其生成的代码性能可与专业开发者编写的代码相媲美，从而降低了GPU编程的门槛，提升了研究效率。

item.title: GPT-3 powers the next generation of apps
item.content:encoded: Over 300 applications are delivering GPT-3–powered search, conversation, text completion, and other advanced AI features through our API.

item.title: Multimodal neurons in artificial neural networks
item.content:encoded: We’ve discovered neurons in CLIP that respond to the same concept whether presented literally, symbolically, or conceptually. This may explain CLIP’s accuracy in classifying surprising visual renditions of concepts, and is also an important step toward understanding the associations and biases that CLIP and similar models learn.

item.title: Understanding the capabilities, limitations, and societal impact of large language models
item.content:encoded: Understanding the capabilities, limitations, and societal impact of large language models

item.title: Scaling Kubernetes to 7,500 nodes
item.content:encoded: We’ve scaled Kubernetes clusters to 7,500 nodes, producing a scalable infrastructure for large models like GPT-3, CLIP, and DALL·E, but also for rapid small-scale iterative research such as Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models.

item.title: DALL·E: Creating images from text
item.content:encoded: We’ve trained a neural network called DALL·E that creates images from text captions for a wide range of concepts expressible in natural language.

item.title: CLIP: Connecting text and images
item.content:encoded: We’re introducing a neural network called CLIP which efficiently learns visual concepts from natural language supervision. CLIP can be applied to any visual classification benchmark by simply providing the names of the visual categories to be recognized, similar to the “zero-shot” capabilities of GPT-2 and GPT-3.

item.content:encoded: It’s been a year of dramatic change and growth at OpenAI.

item.content:encoded: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research.

item.content:encoded:
ChatGPT Pro 用户可安全连接财务账户，获取AI驱动的财务建议。



摘要： ChatGPT 为美国 Pro 用户推出一项新的个人财务管理功能，允许用户安全连接其金融账户，获得基于自身财务状况、目标和优先事项的 AI 驱动洞察与指导。该功能旨在提升用户的财务管理体验，提供更个性化的财务建议，帮助用户更好地规划和管理财务。

item.title: 基于像素序列的生成模型在图像补全与采样中的表现
item.content:encoded:
Transformer模型可生成高质量图像补全与样本。



摘要： 研究发现，与语言模型类似，训练用于像素序列的Transformer模型也能生成连贯的图像补全和样本。通过建立样本质量与图像分类准确率之间的关联，研究显示该生成模型在无监督设置下具备与顶级卷积网络相竞争的特征。

item.title: AI算法进步显著超越硬件效率
item.content:encoded:
AI训练计算量每16个月减半，算法进步优于硬件提升。



摘要： 研究显示，自2012年以来，训练神经网络达到ImageNet分类相同性能所需的计算量每16个月减少一半。与2012年相比，现在训练神经网络的计算量仅为AlexNet时代的1/44，而摩尔定律仅能带来11倍的效率提升。这表明在近期获得大量投资的AI任务中，算法进步带来的收益超过了传统硬件效率的提升。

item.title: 深度学习模型中的双下降现象研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习模型在增大时出现双下降现象，需进一步研究。



摘要： 本文研究了深度学习模型中普遍存在的双下降现象，发现CNN、ResNet和Transformer等模型在模型规模、数据量或训练时间增加时，性能先提升、后下降、再提升。这种现象通常通过仔细的正则化方法避免。尽管该现象具有普遍性，但其原因尚未完全明确，作者认为这是未来重要的研究方向。

item.title: GPT-2最大版本发布及模型检测支持
item.content:encoded:
GPT-2最大版本发布，用于模型检测与社区测试。



摘要： 作为GPT-2分阶段发布的最终版本，此次发布了包含15亿参数的最大模型，并提供了代码和模型权重，以帮助检测GPT-2模型的输出。尽管自8月以来已有更大语言模型发布，但团队仍按原计划进行分阶段发布，以提供完整的分阶段发布案例。该案例旨在为未来强大模型的开发者提供参考，并继续与AI社区探讨负责任的发布方式。

item.title: 神经网络与仿人机械手解决魔方难题
item.content:encoded:
神经网络结合仿人机械手成功解决魔方，展示强化学习在现实世界的应用。



摘要： 研究人员训练了一对神经网络，使其能够通过仿人机械手解决魔方问题。这些神经网络完全在模拟环境中训练，使用了与OpenAI Five相同的强化学习代码，并结合了新的自动领域随机化技术（ADR）。该系统能够应对训练中未遇到的情况，例如被一只毛绒长颈鹿触碰。这表明强化学习不仅适用于虚拟任务，也能解决需要高度灵活性的现实世界问题。

item.title: OpenAI Scholars 2020: Applications open
item.content:encoded: We are now accepting applications for our third class of OpenAI Scholars.

item.title: 基于人类反馈的GPT-2模型微调与摘要任务研究
item.content:encoded:
通过人类反馈微调GPT-2模型，提升其与人类偏好的一致性。



摘要： 文章介绍了通过人类反馈对774M参数的GPT-2语言模型进行微调，以匹配外部人类标注者的偏好。尽管这些偏好并不总是与研究者自身一致，但在摘要任务中，标注者更倾向于直接复制输入中的句子，因此模型也学会了这种复制行为。摘要任务需要60,000个标注数据，而其他较简单的文本续写任务仅需5,000个标注。研究的目标是将安全技术推向“机器与人类交流”的通用任务，以更好地理解人类价值观。

item.title: 智能体在躲藏游戏中演化出复杂策略
item.content:encoded:
智能体在躲藏游戏中发展出六种复杂策略，展现自监督学习的潜力。



摘要： 研究发现，在一个模拟的躲藏游戏环境中，智能体通过训练逐步发展出六种不同的策略和反策略，其中一些策略是研究人员未曾预料到的。这种自监督的复杂性表明，多智能体之间的相互适应可能在未来催生高度复杂和智能的行为。

item.title: 新型评估方法提升神经网络对抗攻击防御能力
item.content:encoded:
新方法评估神经网络对抗攻击防御能力，提出UAR指标。



摘要： 本文提出一种新方法，用于评估神经网络分类器在未见过的对抗攻击下的可靠性。该方法引入了UAR（Unforeseen Attack Robustness）指标，用以衡量单个模型在面对意外攻击时的鲁棒性，并强调需要在更广泛的未预见攻击场景中测试模型性能。

item.title: GPT-2模型发布及合作规范更新
item.content:encoded:
GPT-2模型发布，同时提供法律协议促进模型共享。



摘要： 文章介绍了GPT-2语言模型的发布，该模型拥有7.74亿参数，此前已陆续发布小规模和中等规模版本。文章还提到与AI社区合作研究模型的潜在滥用风险和社会效益，并发布了一个开源法律协议以促进组织间的模型共享。此外，还发表了一篇技术报告，介绍在AI研究界协调发布规范的经验。

item.title: Learning Day
item.content:encoded: At OpenAI, each Thursday is Learning Day: a day where employees have the option to self-study technical skills that will make them better at their job but which aren’t being learned from daily work.

item.title: 微软投资10亿美元支持OpenAI开发通用人工智能
item.content:encoded:
微软投资10亿美元支持OpenAI开发AGI并扩展Azure AI能力。



摘要： 微软宣布向OpenAI投资10亿美元，以支持其构建具有广泛经济利益的人工通用智能（AGI）。双方将合作在Microsoft Azure中开发软硬件平台，以支持AGI的发展。同时，微软将与OpenAI共同研发新的Azure AI超算技术，并成为其独家云服务提供商，进一步提升Azure在大规模AI系统中的能力。

item.title: 推动AI安全规范长期合作的四大策略
item.content:encoded:
文章提出四大策略促进AI行业长期合作提升安全性。



摘要： 本文是一篇政策研究论文，提出了四种当前可采取的策略，以提高AI行业中长期合作制定安全规范的可能性。这些策略包括沟通风险与利益、技术协作、增加透明度以及激励标准制定。分析表明，行业在安全方面的合作对于确保AI系统的安全和有益至关重要，但竞争压力可能导致集体行动问题，使AI公司对安全投入不足。作者希望这些建议能促进AI安全发展的更广泛合作，带来更好的全球成果。

item.title: OpenAI Robotics Symposium 2019
item.content:encoded: We hosted the first OpenAI Robotics Symposium on April 27, 2019.

item.title: OpenAI Scholars 2019: Final projects
item.content:encoded: Our second class of OpenAI Scholars has concluded, with all eight scholars producing an exciting final project showcased at Scholars Demo Day at OpenAI.

item.title: OpenAI Fellows Fall 2018: Final projects
item.content:encoded: Our second class of OpenAI Fellows has wrapped up, with each Fellow going from a machine learning beginner to core OpenAI contributor in the course of a 6-month apprenticeship. We are currently reviewing applications on a rolling basis for our next round of OpenAI Fellows Summer 2019.

item.title: Transfer of adversarial robustness between perturbation types
item.content:encoded: Transfer of adversarial robustness between perturbation types

item.title: MuseNet
item.content:encoded: We’ve created MuseNet, a deep neural network that can generate 4-minute musical compositions with 10 different instruments, and can combine styles from country to Mozart to the Beatles. MuseNet was not explicitly programmed with our understanding of music, but instead discovered patterns of harmony, rhythm, and style by learning to predict the next token in hundreds of thousands of MIDI files. MuseNet uses the same general-purpose unsupervised technology as GPT-2, a large-scale transformer model trained to predict the next token in a sequence, whether audio or text.

item.title: Generative modeling with sparse transformers
item.content:encoded: We’ve developed the Sparse Transformer, a deep neural network which sets new records at predicting what comes next in a sequence—whether text, images, or sound. It uses an algorithmic improvement of the attention mechanism to extract patterns from sequences 30x longer than possible previously.

item.title: OpenAI Five defeats Dota 2 world champions
item.content:encoded: OpenAI Five is the first AI to beat the world champions in an esports game, having won two back-to-back games versus the world champion Dota 2 team, OG, at Finals this weekend. Both OpenAI Five and DeepMind’s AlphaStar had previously beaten good pros privately but lost their live pro matches, making this also the first time an AI has beaten esports pros on livestream.

item.title: OpenAI Five Finals
item.content:encoded: We’ll be holding our final live event for OpenAI Five at 11:30am PT on April 13.

item.title: Implicit generation and generalization methods for energy-based models
item.content:encoded: We’ve made progress towards stable and scalable training of energy-based models (EBMs) resulting in better sample quality and generalization ability than existing models. Generation in EBMs spends more compute to continually refine its answers and doing so can generate samples competitive with GANs at low temperatures, while also having mode coverage guarantees of likelihood-based models. We hope these findings stimulate further research into this promising class of models.

item.title: OpenAI Scholars 2019: Meet our Scholars
item.content:encoded: Our class of eight scholars (out of 550 applicants) brings together collective expertise in literature, philosophy, cell biology, statistics, economics, quantum physics, and business innovation.

item.title: OpenAI LP
item.content:encoded: We’ve created OpenAI LP, a new “capped-profit” company that allows us to rapidly increase our investments in compute and talent while including checks and balances to actualize our mission.

item.title: Introducing Activation Atlases
item.content:encoded: We’ve created activation atlases (in collaboration with Google researchers), a new technique for visualizing what interactions between neurons can represent. As AI systems are deployed in increasingly sensitive contexts, having a better understanding of their internal decision-making processes will let us identify weaknesses and investigate failures.

item.title: How Omio is building the future of conversational travel
item.content:encoded: Discover how Omio uses OpenAI to power conversational travel experiences, accelerate product development, and transform into an AI-native company.

item.title: Work with Codex from anywhere
item.content:encoded: Use Codex anywhere with the ChatGPT mobile app. Monitor, steer, and approve coding tasks in real time across devices and remote environments.

item.title: Helping ChatGPT better recognize context in sensitive conversations
item.content:encoded: Learn how new ChatGPT safety updates improve context awareness in sensitive conversations, helping detect risk over time and respond more safely.

item.title: 发布神经元多智能体游戏环境用于强化学习
item.content:encoded:
发布神经元多智能体环境以提升强化学习探索能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了发布的一个神经元多智能体（Neural MMO）平台，这是一个支持大量可变数量智能体的开放性任务环境。该平台通过引入多种智能体和物种，促进了更广泛的探索、分化生态位的形成，并提高了整体智能体的适应能力。该环境为研究多智能体协作与竞争提供了新的实验基础。

item.title: AI安全研究需社会科学家参与以确保与人类价值观对齐
item.content:encoded:
AI安全研究需社会科学家参与，以确保算法与人类价值观一致。



摘要： 本文主张长期AI安全研究需要社会科学家的参与，以确保AI系统在实际人类使用中能够正确对齐。文章指出，要使先进AI系统与人类价值观一致，必须解决与人类理性、情感和偏见相关的诸多不确定性。作者希望推动机器学习与社会科学领域的进一步合作，并计划在OpenAI全职招聘社会科学家开展相关研究。

item.title: 大规模无监督语言模型的训练与应用
item.content:encoded:
无需任务训练即可完成多种语言任务。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种大规模无监督语言模型，该模型能够生成连贯的段落文本，并在多个语言建模基准测试中取得最先进的性能。此外，它还能进行基础的阅读理解、机器翻译、问答和摘要任务，而无需进行特定任务的训练。这表明该模型具备强大的泛化能力和多任务处理能力，为自然语言处理领域带来了新的突破。

item.title: 梯度噪声尺度预测神经网络训练的并行性
item.content:encoded:
梯度噪声尺度可预测神经网络训练的并行性，有助于提升AI系统性能。



摘要： 文章指出，梯度噪声尺度作为一种简单的统计指标，能够有效预测神经网络在多种任务中的训练并行性。复杂任务通常具有更嘈杂的梯度，因此未来使用更大的批量尺寸可能更加有效，从而突破AI系统进一步发展的潜在限制。研究还表明，神经网络训练并非神秘的艺术，而是可以被系统化和规范化的过程。

item.content:encoded:
CoinRun是用于评估强化学习算法泛化能力的新环境。



摘要： CoinRun是一个新的训练环境，旨在衡量智能体在新情境中的泛化能力。它在复杂度上介于传统平台游戏和简单任务之间，为最先进的算法提供了有价值的泛化挑战。该环境已帮助解决强化学习领域的一个长期谜题，具有重要的研究价值。

item.content:encoded:
Spinning Up 是一个深度强化学习教育资源，帮助学习者掌握实践技能。

item.title: 基于能量的模型快速学习空间概念并实现跨领域迁移
item.content:encoded:
模型通过少量示例学习空间概念并应用于三维机器人任务。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于能量的模型，能够快速学习识别和生成如“靠近”、“在上方”、“最远”等空间概念，这些概念以二维点集形式表达。该模型仅需五次示例即可掌握这些概念，并展示了跨领域的迁移能力——在二维粒子环境中学习的概念可成功应用于三维物理机器人任务中。

item.title: 基于好奇心的强化学习探索方法RND在蒙特祖玛复仇中的应用
item.content:encoded:
RND方法通过好奇心提升强化学习探索能力，超越人类表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了Random Network Distillation (RND) 方法，这是一种基于预测的强化学习探索机制，旨在通过激发代理的 curiosity（好奇心）来增强其环境探索能力。该方法首次在经典游戏 Montezuma’s Revenge 中实现了超越平均人类表现的成果，展示了其在复杂环境中有效探索的能力。

item.title: 迭代增强：一种新的AI安全技术
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的AI安全技术，通过分解任务实现复杂目标。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为“迭代增强”的AI安全技术，该技术通过将复杂任务分解为更简单的子任务来实现超出人类能力范围的行为和目标，而不是依赖标注数据或奖励函数。尽管该方法仍处于早期阶段，且仅在简单的算法领域进行了实验，但作者认为其具有可扩展性，可能为AI安全提供有效解决方案。

Glow是一种可逆生成模型，支持高效采样和高分辨率图像生成。

item.title: 基于人类示范的强化学习算法在《蒙特祖马的复仇》中取得新高分
item.content:encoded:
基于人类示范的PPO算法在《蒙特祖马的复仇》中获得74500分，创历史新高。



摘要： 研究人员训练了一个智能体，通过单一的人类示范在《蒙特祖马的复仇》游戏中获得了74,500分，这一成绩优于之前所有已发表的结果。该算法的核心是让智能体从示范中选择合适的初始状态进行游戏，并利用PPO（近端策略优化）算法优化游戏得分。PPO是一种广泛用于强化学习的算法，也是OpenAI Five所采用的技术。这项研究展示了基于人类示范的强化学习方法在复杂游戏中的潜力。

item.title: OpenAI Five
item.content:encoded: Our team of five neural networks, OpenAI Five, has started to defeat amateur human teams at Dota 2.

item.title: Retro Contest: Results
item.content:encoded: The first run of our Retro Contest—exploring the development of algorithms that can generalize from previous experience—is now complete.

item.title: Learning policy representations in multiagent systems
item.content:encoded: Learning policy representations in multiagent systems

item.title: Improving language understanding with unsupervised learning
item.content:encoded: We’ve obtained state-of-the-art results on a suite of diverse language tasks with a scalable, task-agnostic system, which we’re also releasing. Our approach is a combination of two existing ideas: transformers and unsupervised pre-training. These results provide a convincing example that pairing supervised learning methods with unsupervised pre-training works very well; this is an idea that many have explored in the past, and we hope our result motivates further research into applying this idea on larger and more diverse datasets.

item.title: GamePad: A learning environment for theorem proving
item.content:encoded: GamePad: A learning environment for theorem proving

item.title: OpenAI Fellows Fall 2018
item.content:encoded: We’re now accepting applications for the next cohort of OpenAI Fellows, a program which offers a compensated 6-month apprenticeship in AI research at OpenAI.

item.title: Gym Retro
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing the full version of Gym Retro, a platform for reinforcement learning research on games. This brings our publicly-released game count from around 70 Atari games and 30 Sega games to over 1,000 games across a variety of backing emulators. We’re also releasing the tool we use to add new games to the platform.

item.title: AI and compute
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing an analysis showing that since 2012, the amount of compute used in the largest AI training runs has been increasing exponentially with a 3.4-month doubling time (by comparison, Moore’s Law had a 2-year doubling period)[^footnote-correction]. Since 2012, this metric has grown by more than 300,000x (a 2-year doubling period would yield only a 7x increase). Improvements in compute have been a key component of AI progress, so as long as this trend continues, it’s worth preparing for the implications of systems far outside today’s capabilities.

item.title: AI safety via debate
item.content:encoded: We’re proposing an AI safety technique which trains agents to debate topics with one another, using a human to judge who wins.

item.title: Evolved Policy Gradients
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing an experimental metalearning approach called Evolved Policy Gradients, a method that evolves the loss function of learning agents, which can enable fast training on novel tasks. Agents trained with EPG can succeed at basic tasks at test time that were outside their training regime, like learning to navigate to an object on a different side of the room from where it was placed during training.

item.title: Gotta Learn Fast: A new benchmark for generalization in RL
item.content:encoded: Gotta Learn Fast: A new benchmark for generalization in RL

item.title: Retro Contest
item.content:encoded: We’re launching a transfer learning contest that measures a reinforcement learning algorithm’s ability to generalize from previous experience.

item.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale.

item.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review.

item.title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
item.content:encoded: Learn how Jason Liu uses Codex to preserve context, manage complex projects, and help work continue beyond a single prompt.

item.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
item.content:encoded: Samsung Electronics deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, marking one of OpenAI’s largest enterprise AI rollouts.

item.title: Building a safe, effective sandbox to enable Codex on Windows
item.content:encoded: Learn how OpenAI built a safe, effective sandbox to enable Codex on Windows with controlled file access and network limits.

item.title: OpenAI应对TanStack供应链攻击及系统安全更新
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI回应TanStack供应链攻击，说明安全措施与更新要求。



摘要： OpenAI详细说明了其对TanStack‘Mini Shai-Hulud’供应链攻击的应对措施，包括采取的安全保护手段和签名证书的加固。文章还解释了为何macOS用户必须在2026年6月12日前更新OpenAI应用程序。同时，OpenAI概述了此次事件的影响范围，并强调了其在应对不断演变的软件供应链威胁方面的防御强化工作。

item.title: Reptile：一种基于元学习的简单算法
item.content:encoded:
Reptile是一种高效的元学习算法，通过任务采样和梯度下降优化参数。



摘要： Reptile是一种简单的元学习算法，其核心思想是通过反复采样任务、执行随机梯度下降并根据任务最终参数更新初始参数。该算法是Shortest Descent方法在元学习领域的应用，与一阶MAML算法数学上相似，仅需黑盒优化器（如SGD或Adam）即可运行，具有相近的计算效率和性能表现。

item.title: AI技术被滥用的潜在风险与防范措施
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨AI技术可能被恶意利用及应对策略。



摘要： 本文由多个研究机构合作撰写，预测了恶意行为者可能滥用AI技术的方式，并提出了相应的预防和缓解措施。该研究历时近一年，汇集了来自未来人类研究所、存在性风险研究中心、新美国安全中心、电子前沿基金会等机构专家的智慧，旨在识别AI技术带来的潜在威胁并提出解决方案。

item.title: AI相互教学方法提升学习效果
item.content:encoded:
AI通过精选示例相互教学，提升学习效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种让AI通过人类可理解的示例进行相互教学的方法。该方法能自动选择最有效的示例来教授概念，例如用最佳图片描述‘狗’的概念。实验结果表明，该方法在提升AI学习效果方面具有显著成效。

item.content:encoded:
通过神经网络识别词语对应的物体类型。



摘要： 该系统利用神经网络自动判断一个词属于哪一类约100种自动生成的非排他性类别，从而确定该词所指的物体。这种方法无需人工定义类别，能够自动发现并分类物体类型，提高了识别的准确性和效率。

item.content:encoded:
分层强化学习算法提升任务解决效率。

item.title: 基于仿真的机器人控制器实现环境自适应
item.content:encoded:
仿真训练的机器人控制器可实时应对环境变化。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种最新的机器人技术，通过完全在仿真环境中训练的控制器，使机器人能够在物理设备上执行任务时对未预见的环境变化做出反应。与以往的开环系统不同，该技术构建了闭环系统，提升了机器人在动态环境中的适应能力与任务完成效率。

item.title: Meta-learning for wrestling
item.content:encoded: We show that for the task of simulated robot wrestling, a meta-learning agent can learn to quickly defeat a stronger non-meta-learning agent, and also show that the meta-learning agent can adapt to physical malfunction.

item.title: Competitive self-play
item.content:encoded: We’ve found that self-play allows simulated AIs to discover physical skills like tackling, ducking, faking, kicking, catching, and diving for the ball, without explicitly designing an environment with these skills in mind. Self-play ensures that the environment is always the right difficulty for an AI to improve. Taken alongside our Dota 2 self-play results, we have increasing confidence that self-play will be a core part of powerful AI systems in the future.

item.title: Nonlinear computation in deep linear networks
item.content:encoded: Nonlinear computation in deep linear networks

item.title: Learning to model other minds
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing an algorithm which accounts for the fact that other agents are learning too, and discovers self-interested yet collaborative strategies like tit-for-tat in the iterated prisoner’s dilemma. This algorithm, Learning with Opponent-Learning Awareness (LOLA), is a small step towards agents that model other minds.

item.title: Learning with opponent-learning awareness
item.content:encoded: Learning with opponent-learning awareness

item.title: OpenAI Baselines: ACKTR & A2C
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing two new OpenAI Baselines implementations: ACKTR and A2C. A2C is a synchronous, deterministic variant of Asynchronous Advantage Actor Critic (A3C) which we’ve found gives equal performance. ACKTR is a more sample-efficient reinforcement learning algorithm than TRPO and A2C, and requires only slightly more computation than A2C per update.

item.title: More on Dota 2
item.content:encoded: Our Dota 2 result shows that self-play can catapult the performance of machine learning systems from far below human level to superhuman, given sufficient compute. In the span of a month, our system went from barely matching a high-ranked player to beating the top pros and has continued to improve since then. Supervised deep learning systems can only be as good as their training datasets, but in self-play systems, the available data improves automatically as the agent gets better.

item.title: Dota 2
item.content:encoded: We’ve created a bot which beats the world’s top professionals at 1v1 matches of Dota 2 under standard tournament rules. The bot learned the game from scratch by self-play, and does not use imitation learning or tree search. This is a step towards building AI systems which accomplish well-defined goals in messy, complicated situations involving real humans.

item.title: Gathering human feedback
item.content:encoded: RL-Teacher is an open-source implementation of our interface to train AIs via occasional human feedback rather than hand-crafted reward functions. The underlying technique was developed as a step towards safe AI systems, but also applies to reinforcement learning problems with rewards that are hard to specify.

item.title: Better exploration with parameter noise
item.content:encoded: We’ve found that adding adaptive noise to the parameters of reinforcement learning algorithms frequently boosts performance. This exploration method is simple to implement and very rarely decreases performance, so it’s worth trying on any problem.

item.title: Proximal Policy Optimization
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing a new class of reinforcement learning algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), which perform comparably or better than state-of-the-art approaches while being much simpler to implement and tune. PPO has become the default reinforcement learning algorithm at OpenAI because of its ease of use and good performance.

item.content:encoded: See how finance teams can use ChatGPT Work to build MBRs, reporting packs, variance bridges, model checks, and planning scenarios from real work inputs.

item.title: 图像欺骗神经网络分类器，挑战自动驾驶安全论断
item.content:encoded:
研究展示图像可从不同角度欺骗神经网络，挑战自动驾驶安全观点。



摘要： 研究生成的图像能够可靠地欺骗神经网络分类器，无论从何种尺度和视角观看。这一发现反驳了近期关于自动驾驶汽车难以被恶意欺骗的观点，因为这些系统通常会从多个角度和尺度捕捉图像。该研究揭示了神经网络在面对多视角输入时仍可能受到攻击，对自动驾驶系统的安全性提出了新的质疑。

item.title: 通过比较行为推断人类意图的AI算法
item.content:encoded:
AI系统通过比较行为推断人类目标，提升安全性。



摘要： 为了构建更安全的AI系统，研究人员致力于减少人类编写目标函数的需求。由于复杂的任务目标难以准确表达，错误的代理目标可能导致危险行为。为此，与DeepMind安全团队合作开发了一种算法，该算法通过对比两种行为的优劣来推断人类的真实意图，从而提高AI系统的安全性和适应性。

item.title: 多智能体环境在AGI发展中的作用
item.content:encoded:
多智能体环境推动AGI发展，具有自然课程和动态挑战。



摘要： 多智能体环境为AGI的发展提供了重要的研究场景。这类环境具有两个显著特点：一是存在自然的课程体系，环境难度由竞争者的技能水平决定；二是没有稳定的平衡点，无论智能体多聪明，总会有持续进化的压力。与传统环境相比，多智能体环境更具动态性和挑战性，需要更多研究来掌握其特性。

item.title: 进化策略在强化学习中的表现与优势
item.content:encoded:
进化策略在强化学习中表现出色且更便捷。



摘要： 研究发现，进化策略（ES）作为一种已有数十年历史的优化技术，在现代强化学习基准测试（如Atari和MuJoCo）中表现可与标准强化学习方法媲美，同时克服了后者的一些不便之处。ES在性能上具有竞争力，且在实际应用中可能更加高效和易于实现。

item.title: 深度学习基础设施的重要性与开源生态的作用
item.content:encoded:
深度学习依赖高质量基础设施，开源生态助力构建高效系统。



摘要： 文章指出，深度学习是一门经验科学，团队的基础设施质量对进展有显著影响。得益于当前的开源生态系统，任何人都可以构建强大的深度学习基础设施，从而推动技术发展。文章强调了基础设施在深度学习中的关键作用，并肯定了开源社区对技术普及和创新的贡献。

item.title: Machine Learning Unconference
item.content:encoded: The latest information about the Unconference is now available at the Unconference wiki, which will be periodically updated with more information for attendees.

item.title: Team update
item.content:encoded: We’ve hired more great people to help us achieve our goals. Welcome, everyone!

item.title: Special projects
item.content:encoded: Impactful scientific work requires working on the right problems—problems which are not just interesting, but whose solutions matter.

item.title: Concrete AI safety problems
item.content:encoded: We (along with researchers from Berkeley and Stanford) are co-authors on today’s paper led by Google Brain researchers, Concrete Problems in AI Safety. The paper explores many research problems around ensuring that modern machine learning systems operate as intended.

item.title: OpenAI technical goals
item.content:encoded: OpenAI’s mission is to build safe AI, and ensure AI’s benefits are as widely and evenly distributed as possible.

item.title: Generative models
item.content:encoded: This post describes four projects that share a common theme of enhancing or using generative models, a branch of unsupervised learning techniques in machine learning. In addition to describing our work, this post will tell you a bit more about generative models: what they are, why they are important, and where they might be going.

item.title: Adversarial training methods for semi-supervised text classification
item.content:encoded: Adversarial training methods for semi-supervised text classification

item.title: Team update
item.content:encoded: We’d like to welcome the latest set of team members to OpenAI (and we’re still hiring!)

item.title: OpenAI Gym Beta
item.content:encoded: We’re releasing the public beta of OpenAI Gym, a toolkit for developing and comparing reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms. It consists of a growing suite of environments (from simulated robots to Atari games), and a site for comparing and reproducing results.

item.title: Welcome, Pieter and Shivon!
item.content:encoded: We have two more team updates.

item.title: Team++
item.content:encoded: We've had some fantastic people join over the past few months (and we're still hiring). Welcome, everyone!

item.title: Weight normalization: A simple reparameterization to accelerate training of deep neural networks
item.content:encoded: Weight normalization: A simple reparameterization to accelerate training of deep neural networks

item.title: Introducing OpenAI
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is a non-profit artificial intelligence research company. Our goal is to advance digital intelligence in the way that is most likely to benefit humanity as a whole, unconstrained by a need to generate financial return. Since our research is free from financial obligations, we can better focus on a positive human impact.

item.title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, helping organizations manage costs and scale AI with confidence.

item.title: Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT
item.content:encoded: Learn how GPT-5.5 Instant improves ChatGPT’s health and wellness responses with stronger reasoning, better context, clearer communication, and physician-informed evaluations.

item.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
item.content:encoded: Researchers used an OpenAI reasoning model to help diagnose rare diseases, identifying 18 new diagnoses in previously unsolved cases.

item.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

item.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.

item.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
item.content:encoded: Learn how Symphony, an open-source spec for Codex orchestration, turns issue trackers into always-on agent systems—boosting engineering output and reducing context switching.

item.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
item.content:encoded: How Choco used OpenAI APIs to streamline food distribution, boost productivity, and unlock growth—an in-depth customer story on real-world AI impact.

item.title: Our principles
item.content:encoded: Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. Sam Altman shares five principles that guide our work.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
item.content:encoded: Introducing GPT-5.5, our smartest model yet—faster, more capable, and built for complex tasks like coding, research, and data analysis across tools.

item.title: GPT-5.5 System Card
item.content:encoded: GPT-5.5 System Card

item.title: ChatGPT Codex settings
item.content:encoded: Learn how to configure Codex settings, including personalization, detail level, and permissions, to run tasks smoothly and customize your workflow.

item.title: How to get started with ChatGPT Codex
item.content:encoded: Learn how to get started with ChatGPT Codex by setting up projects, creating threads, and completing your first tasks with step-by-step guidance.

item.title: Working with ChatGPT Codex
item.content:encoded: Learn how to set up your ChatGPT Codex workspace, create threads and projects, manage files, and start completing tasks with step-by-step guidance.

item.title: Automations
item.content:encoded: Learn how to automate tasks in ChatGPT Codex using schedules and triggers to create reports, summaries, and recurring workflows without manual effort.

item.title: Plugins and skills
item.content:encoded: Learn how to use ChatGPT Codex plugins and skills to connect tools, access data, and follow repeatable workflows to automate tasks and improve results.

item.title: What is ChatGPT Codex?
item.content:encoded: Learn how ChatGPT Codex helps you go beyond chat by automating tasks, connecting tools, and producing real outputs like docs and dashboards.

item.title: Making ChatGPT better for clinicians
item.content:encoded: OpenAI makes ChatGPT for Clinicians free for verified U.S. physicians, nurse practitioners, and pharmacists, supporting clinical care, documentation, and research.

item.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
item.content:encoded: A deep dive into the Codex agent loop, showing how WebSockets and connection-scoped caching reduced API overhead and improved model latency.

item.title: Workspace agents
item.content:encoded: Learn how to build, use, and scale workspace agents in ChatGPT to automate repeatable workflows, connect tools, and streamline team operations.

item.title: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT
item.content:encoded: Workspace agents in ChatGPT are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
item.content:encoded: OpenAI Privacy Filter is an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information (PII) in text with state-of-the-art accuracy

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Images 2.0
item.content:encoded: ChatGPT Images 2.0 introduces a state-of-the-art image generation model with improved text rendering, multilingual support, and advanced visual reasoning.

item.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
item.content:encoded: OpenAI launches Codex Labs, partners with with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle, and hits 4M Codex WAU.

item.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
item.content:encoded: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

item.title: Codex for (almost) everything
item.content:encoded: The updated Codex app for macOS and Windows adds computer use, in-app browsing, image generation, memory, and plugins to accelerate developer workflows.

item.title: Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces GPT-Rosalind, a frontier reasoning model built to accelerate drug discovery, genomics analysis, protein reasoning, and scientific research workflows.

item.title: Accelerating the cyber defense ecosystem that protects us all
item.content:encoded: Leading security firms and enterprises join OpenAI’s Trusted Access for Cyber, using GPT-5.4-Cyber and $10M in API grants to strengthen global cyber defense.

item.title: The next evolution of the Agents SDK
item.content:encoded: OpenAI updates the Agents SDK with native sandbox execution and a model-native harness, helping developers build secure, long-running agents across files and tools.

item.title: Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
item.content:encoded: OpenAI expands its Trusted Access for Cyber program, introducing GPT-5.4-Cyber to vetted defenders and strengthening safeguards as AI cybersecurity capabilities advance.

item.title: Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI
item.content:encoded: Cloudflare brings OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 and Codex to Agent Cloud, enabling enterprises to build, deploy, and scale AI agents for real-world tasks with speed and security.

item.title: Research with ChatGPT
item.content:encoded: Learn how to research with ChatGPT using search and deep research to find up-to-date information, analyze sources, and generate structured insights.

item.title: Our response to the Axios developer tool compromise
item.content:encoded: OpenAI responds to the Axios supply chain attack by rotating macOS code signing certificates, updating apps, and confirming no user data was compromised.

item.content:encoded: Learn how to use ChatGPT for research to gather sources, analyze information, and create structured, citation-backed insights.

item.content:encoded: Discover how leaders can build AI-ready organizations using clear strategy, training, governance, and accelerated innovation.

item.title: A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.

item.title: Introducing LifeSciBench
item.content:encoded: Introducing LifeSciBench, an expert-authored, expert-reviewed benchmark for evaluating how AI systems handle real-world life science research tasks and decisions.

item.title: Industrial policy for the Intelligence Age
item.content:encoded: Explore our ambitious, people-first industrial policy ideas for the AI era—focused on expanding opportunity, sharing prosperity, and building resilient institutions as advanced intelligence evolves.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI outlines the next phase of enterprise AI, as adoption accelerates across industries with Frontier, ChatGPT Enterprise, Codex, and company-wide AI agents.

item.content:encoded: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.

item.content:encoded: The OpenAI Foundation announces plans to invest at least $1 billion in curing diseases, economic opportunity, AI resilience, and community programs.

item.title: Creating with Sora Safely
item.content:encoded: To address the novel safety challenges posed by a state-of-the-art video model as well as a new social creation platform, we’ve built Sora 2 and the Sora app with safety at the foundation. Our approach is anchored in concrete protections.

item.title: How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment

item.content:encoded: GPT-5.4 mini and nano are smaller, faster versions of GPT-5.4 optimized for coding, tool use, multimodal reasoning, and high-volume API and sub-agent workloads.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI Japan announces the Japan Teen Safety Blueprint, introducing stronger age protections, parental controls, and well-being safeguards for teens using generative AI.

item.content:encoded: New research shows Americans send nearly 3 million daily messages to ChatGPT asking about compensation and earnings, helping close the wage information gap.

item.content:encoded: A deep dive into why Codex Security doesn’t rely on traditional SAST, instead using AI-driven constraint reasoning and validation to find real vulnerabilities with fewer false positives.

item.content:encoded: How ChatGPT defends against prompt injection and social engineering by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data in agent workflows.

item.content:encoded: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

item.content:encoded: IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction hierarchy, safety steerability, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

item.content:encoded: Using OpenAI reasoning models, Descript unlocked automatic localization of large content libraries without losing timing or meaning.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces ChatGPT for Excel and new financial app integrations, powered by GPT-5.4 to accelerate modeling, research, and analysis in regulated environments.

item.title: Extending single-minus amplitudes to gravitons
item.content:encoded: A new preprint extends single-minus amplitudes to gravitons, with GPT-5.2 Pro helping derive and verify nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces the Learning Outcomes Measurement Suite to assess AI’s impact on student learning across diverse educational environments over time.

item.content:encoded: Axios COO Allison Murphy explains how the company uses AI to support local reporters, streamline newsroom workflows, and deliver high-impact local journalism at scale.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Amazon announce a strategic partnership bringing OpenAI’s Frontier platform to AWS, expanding AI infrastructure, custom models, and enterprise AI agents.

item.title: Scaling AI for everyone
item.content:encoded: Today we’re announcing $110B in new investment at a $730B pre money valuation. This includes $30B from SoftBank, $30B from NVIDIA, and $50B from Amazon.

item.content:encoded: Microsoft and OpenAI continue to work closely across research, engineering, and product development, building on years of deep collaboration and shared success.

item.content:encoded: Stateful Runtime for Agents in Amazon Bedrock brings persistent orchestration, memory, and secure execution to multi-step AI workflows powered by OpenAI.

item.content:encoded:
AI编码代理可提升联邦许可效率，减少NEPA起草时间15%。

item.title: OpenAI Codex and Figma launch seamless code-to-design experience

item.title: Disrupting malicious uses of AI | February 2026

item.title: Arvind KC appointed Chief People Officer
item.content:encoded: OpenAI appoints Arvind KC as Chief People Officer to help scale the company, strengthen its culture, and lead how work evolves in the age of AI.

item.title: Why we no longer evaluate SWE-bench Verified
item.content:encoded: SWE-bench Verified is increasingly contaminated and mismeasures frontier coding progress. Our analysis shows flawed tests and training leakage. We recommend SWE-bench Pro.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI announces Frontier Alliance Partners to help enterprises move from AI pilots to production with secure, scalable agent deployments.

item.content:encoded: A new preprint shows GPT-5.2 proposing a new formula for a gluon amplitude, later formally proved and verified by OpenAI and academic collaborators.

item.content:encoded: Introducing Lockdown Mode and Elevated Risk labels in ChatGPT to help organizations defend against prompt injection and AI-driven data exfiltration.

item.content:encoded: How OpenAI built a real-time access system combining rate limits, usage tracking, and credits to power continuous access to Sora and Codex.

item.content:encoded: By focusing on people, not pilots, the Bundesliga club is scaling efficiency, creativity, and knowledge—without losing its football identity.

item.content:encoded: Introducing GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark—our first real-time coding model. 15x faster generation, 128k context, now in research preview for ChatGPT Pro users.

item.title: Making AI work for everyone, everywhere: our approach to localization
item.content:encoded: OpenAI shares its approach to AI localization, showing how globally shared frontier models can be adapted to local languages, laws, and cultures without compromising safety.

item.title: GPT-5 lowers the cost of cell-free protein synthesis
item.content:encoded: An autonomous lab combining OpenAI’s GPT-5 with Ginkgo Bioworks’ cloud automation cut cell-free protein synthesis costs by 40% through closed-loop experimentation.

item.title: Introducing Trusted Access for Cyber
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces Trusted Access for Cyber, a trust-based framework that expands access to frontier cyber capabilities while strengthening safeguards against misuse.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI Frontier
item.content:encoded: OpenAI Frontier is an enterprise platform for building, deploying, and managing AI agents with shared context, onboarding, permissions, and governance.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.3-Codex
item.content:encoded: GPT-5.3-Codex is a Codex-native agent that pairs frontier coding performance with general reasoning to support long-horizon, real-world technical work.

item.title: GPT-5.3-Codex System Card
item.content:encoded: GPT‑5.3-Codex is the most capable agentic coding model to date, combining the frontier coding performance of GPT‑5.2-Codex with the reasoning and professional knowledge capabilities of GPT‑5.2.

item.title: Unlocking the Codex harness: how we built the App Server
item.content:encoded: Learn how to embed the Codex agent using the Codex App Server, a bidirectional JSON-RPC API powering streaming progress, tool use, approvals, and diffs.

item.title: Snowflake and OpenAI partner to bring frontier intelligence to enterprise data
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Snowflake partner in a $200M agreement to bring frontier intelligence into enterprise data, enabling AI agents and insights directly in Snowflake.

item.title: Introducing the Codex app
item.content:encoded: Introducing the Codex app for macOS—a command center for AI coding and software development with multiple agents, parallel workflows, and long-running tasks.

item.title: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent
item.content:encoded: How OpenAI built an in-house AI data agent that uses GPT-5, Codex, and memory to reason over massive datasets and deliver reliable insights in minutes.

item.title: GPT-5及部分模型将于2026年2月13日退役
item.content:encoded:
GPT-5及多个模型将在2026年2月13日退役。



摘要： 2026年2月13日，除了已宣布的GPT-5（Instant、Thinking和Pro）退役外，GPT-4o、GPT-4.1、GPT-4.1 mini和OpenAI o4-mini也将从ChatGPT中退役。目前API未发生任何变化。

item.title: Taisei Corporation shapes the next generation of talent with AI
item.content:encoded: Taisei Corporation’s HR team is leading the rollout of ChatGPT Enterprise to drive AI-powered talent development across the organization.

item.title: The next chapter for AI in the EU
item.content:encoded: OpenAI launches the EU Economic Blueprint 2.0 with new data, partnerships, and initiatives to accelerate AI adoption, skills, and growth across Europe.

item.title: EMEA Youth & Wellbeing Grant
item.content:encoded: Apply for the EMEA Youth & Wellbeing Grant, a €500,000 program funding NGOs and researchers advancing youth safety and wellbeing in the age of AI.

item.title: Keeping your data safe when an AI agent clicks a link
item.content:encoded: Learn how OpenAI protects user data when AI agents open links, preventing URL-based data exfiltration and prompt injection with built-in safeguards.

item.title: PVH reimagines the future of fashion with OpenAI
item.content:encoded: PVH Corp., parent company of Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, is adopting ChatGPT Enterprise to bring AI into fashion design, supply chain, and consumer engagement.

item.title: TRUSTBANK uses AI agents to personalize Furusato Nozei gifts
item.content:encoded: TRUSTBANK partnered with Recursive to build Choice AI using OpenAI models, enabling personalized conversational recommendations that simplify Furusato Nozei gift discovery.

item.title: Introducing Prism
item.content:encoded: Prism is a free LaTeX-native workspace with GPT-5.2 built in, helping researchers write, collaborate, and reason in one place.

item.title: How Indeed uses AI to help evolve the job search
item.content:encoded: Indeed’s CRO Maggie Hulce shares how AI is transforming job search, recruiting, and talent acquisition for employers and job seekers.

item.title: Unrolling the Codex agent loop
item.content:encoded: A technical deep dive into the Codex agent loop, explaining how Codex CLI orchestrates models, tools, prompts, and performance using the Responses API.

item.title: Scaling PostgreSQL to power 800 million ChatGPT users
item.content:encoded: An inside look at how OpenAI scaled PostgreSQL to millions of queries per second using replicas, caching, rate limiting, and workload isolation.

item.title: Inside GPT-5 for Work: How Businesses Use GPT-5
item.content:encoded: A data-driven report on how workers across industries use ChatGPT—covering adoption trends, top tasks, departmental patterns, and the future of AI at work.

item.title: Inside Praktika's conversational approach to language learning
item.content:encoded: How Praktika uses GPT-4.1 and GPT-5.2 to build adaptive AI tutors that personalize lessons, track progress, and help learners achieve real-world language fluency

item.title: How Higgsfield turns simple ideas into cinematic social videos
item.content:encoded: Discover how Higgsfield gives creators cinematic, social-first video output from simple inputs using OpenAI GPT-4.1, GPT-5, and Sora 2.

item.title: How countries can end the capability overhang
item.content:encoded: Our latest report reveals stark differences in advanced AI adoption across countries and outlines new initiatives to help nations capture productivity gains from AI.

item.title: Introducing Edu for Countries
item.content:encoded: Edu for Countries is a new OpenAI initiative helping governments use AI to modernize education systems and build future-ready workforces.

item.title: Horizon 1000: Advancing AI for primary healthcare
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and the Gates Foundation launch Horizon 1000, a $50M pilot advancing AI capabilities for healthcare in Africa. The initiative aims to reach 1,000 clinics by 2028.

item.title: Stargate Community
item.content:encoded: Stargate Community plans detail a community-first approach to AI infrastructure, using locally tailored plans shaped by community input, energy needs, and workforce priorities.

item.title: ServiceNow powers actionable enterprise AI with OpenAI
item.content:encoded: ServiceNow expands access to OpenAI frontier models to power AI-driven enterprise workflows, summarization, search, and voice across the ServiceNow Platform.

item.title: Our approach to age prediction
item.content:encoded: ChatGPT is rolling out age prediction to estimate if accounts are under or over 18, applying safeguards for teens and refining accuracy over time.

item.title: A business that scales with the value of intelligence
item.content:encoded: OpenAI’s business model scales with intelligence—spanning subscriptions, API, ads, commerce, and compute—driven by deepening ChatGPT adoption.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Go, now available worldwide
item.content:encoded: ChatGPT Go is now available worldwide, offering expanded access to GPT-5.2 Instant, higher usage limits, and longer memory—making advanced AI more affordable globally.

item.title: Our approach to advertising and expanding access to ChatGPT
item.content:encoded: OpenAI plans to test advertising in the U.S. for ChatGPT’s free and Go tiers to expand affordable access to AI worldwide, while protecting privacy, trust, and answer quality.

item.title: Investing in Merge Labs
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is investing in Merge Labs to support new brain computer interfaces that bridge biological and artificial intelligence to maximize human ability, agency, and experience.

item.title: The state of enterprise AI
item.content:encoded: A data-driven look at enterprise AI adoption, showing how organizations move from experimentation to real productivity gains and new capabilities.

item.title: Disrupting deceptive uses of AI by covert influence operations
item.content:encoded: We’ve terminated accounts linked to covert influence operations; no significant audience increase due to our services.

item.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
item.content:encoded: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

item.title: Strengthening the U.S. AI supply chain through domestic manufacturing
item.content:encoded: OpenAI launches a new RFP to strengthen the U.S. AI supply chain by accelerating domestic manufacturing, creating jobs, and scaling AI infrastructure.

item.title: OpenAI partners with Cerebras
item.content:encoded: OpenAI partners with Cerebras to add 750MW of high-speed AI compute, reducing inference latency and making ChatGPT faster for real-time AI workloads.

item.title: Zenken通过部署ChatGPT企业版提升销售表现
item.content:encoded:
Zenken部署ChatGPT企业版提升销售效率与客户互动。

item.title: Datadog uses Codex for system-level code review
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Datadog brand graphic with the OpenAI wordmark on the left, the Datadog logo on the right, and a central abstract brown fur-like texture panel on a white background.

item.title: OpenAI for Healthcare
item.content:encoded: OpenAI for Healthcare enables secure, enterprise-grade AI that supports HIPAA compliance—reducing administrative burden and supporting clinical workflows.

item.title: Netomi’s lessons for scaling agentic systems into the enterprise
item.content:encoded: How Netomi scales enterprise AI agents using GPT-4.1 and GPT-5.2—combining concurrency, governance, and multi-step reasoning for reliable production workflows.

item.title: How Tolan builds voice-first AI with GPT-5.1
item.content:encoded: Tolan built a voice-first AI companion with GPT-5.1, combining low-latency responses, real-time context reconstruction, and memory-driven personalities for natural conversations.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Health
item.content:encoded: ChatGPT Health is a dedicated experience that securely connects your health data and apps, with privacy protections and a physician-informed design.

item.title: OpenAI强化ChatGPT Atlas防御提示注入攻击

item.title: GPT-5.2在数学与科学领域取得突破性进展
item.content:encoded:
GPT-5.2在数学和科学任务中表现优异，推动了研究进展。



摘要： GPT-5.2是OpenAI最新推出的最强模型，专门针对数学和科学任务进行了优化，在GPQA Diamond和FrontierMath等基准测试中取得了最先进的成果。该模型不仅提升了算法性能，还在实际研究中实现了突破，包括解决一个开放的理论问题并生成可靠的数学证明，标志着人工智能在科学领域的重大进步。

item.title: OpenAI回顾十年发展与AGI未来展望
item.content:encoded:

摘要： OpenAI回顾了过去十年在人工智能领域的重大突破，从早期研究到如今广泛应用的AI系统，这些成果改变了技术的可能性。文章分享了这十年的经验教训，并表达了对构建造福全人类的通用人工智能（AGI）的乐观态度。OpenAI强调其使命是推动AI技术向积极方向发展，确保其带来的利益惠及全球。

item.title: GPT-5.2模型的安全机制与训练数据概述
item.content:encoded:
GPT-5.2沿用GPT-5系列安全机制，训练数据来源多样。



摘要： GPT-5.2是GPT-5系列的最新模型家族，其全面的安全缓解措施与GPT-5和GPT-5.1系统卡中描述的方法基本一致。与其他OpenAI模型类似，GPT-5.2模型在训练过程中使用了多种数据源，包括互联网上公开的信息、与第三方合作获取的数据，以及用户、人工训练师和研究人员提供的内容。

item.title: The Walt Disney Company and OpenAI reach landmark agreement to bring beloved characters to Sora
item.content:encoded: Disney and OpenAI have reached an agreement to bring more than 200 Disney, Marvel, Pixar and Star Wars characters to Sora for fan-inspired short videos. The agreement emphasizes responsible AI in entertainment and includes Disney’s company-wide use of ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API.

item.title: How Podium is arming 10,000+ SMBs with AI agents
item.content:encoded: Discover how Podium used OpenAI’s GPT-5 to build “Jerry,” an AI teammate driving 300% growth and transforming how Main Street businesses serve customers.

item.title: Strengthening cyber resilience as AI capabilities advance
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is investing in stronger safeguards and defensive capabilities as AI models become more powerful in cybersecurity. We explain how we assess risk, limit misuse, and work with the security community to strengthen cyber resilience.

item.title: How Scout24 is building the next generation of real-estate search with AI
item.content:encoded: Scout24 has created a GPT-5 powered conversational assistant that reimagines real-estate search, guiding users with clarifying questions, summaries, and tailored listing recommendations.

item.title: OpenAI co-founds Agentic AI Foundation, donates AGENTS.md
item.content:encoded: OpenAI co-founds the Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation and donates AGENTS.md to support open, interoperable standards for safe agentic AI.

item.title: Launching our first OpenAI Certifications courses
item.content:encoded: Learn how OpenAI’s new certifications and AI Foundations courses help people build real-world AI skills, boost career opportunities, and prepare for the future of work.

item.title: OpenAI appoints Denise Dresser as Chief Revenue Officer
item.content:encoded: Denise Dresser is joining as Chief Revenue Officer, overseeing OpenAI’s global revenue strategy across enterprise and customer success. She will help more businesses put AI to work in their day-to-day operations as OpenAI continues to scale.

item.title: Bringing powerful AI to millions across Europe with Deutsche Telekom
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is collaborating with Deutsche Telekom to bring advanced, multilingual AI experiences to millions of people across Europe. ChatGPT Enterprise will also be deployed to help employees at Deutsche Telekom improve workflows and accelerate innovation.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank of Australia builds AI fluency at scale
item.content:encoded: Commonwealth Bank of Australia partners with OpenAI to roll out ChatGPT Enterprise to 50,000 employees, building AI fluency at scale to improve customer service and fraud response.

item.title: Instacart and OpenAI partner on AI shopping experiences
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Instacart are deepening their longstanding partnership by bringing the first fully integrated grocery shopping and Instant Checkout payment app to ChatGPT.

item.title: The state of enterprise AI
item.content:encoded: Key findings from OpenAI’s enterprise data show accelerating AI adoption, deeper integration, and measurable productivity gains across industries in 2025.

item.title: How Virgin Atlantic uses AI to enhance every step of travel
item.content:encoded: Virgin Atlantic CFO Oliver Byers shares how the airline is using AI to speed up development, improve decision-making, and elevate customer experience.

item.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
item.content:encoded: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI for Australia
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is launching OpenAI for Australia to build sovereign AI infrastructure, upskill more than 1.5 million workers, and accelerate innovation across the country’s growing AI ecosystem.

item.title: OpenAI to acquire Neptune
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is acquiring Neptune to deepen visibility into model behavior and strengthen the tools researchers use to track experiments and monitor training.

item.title: How confessions can keep language models honest
item.content:encoded: OpenAI researchers are testing “confessions,” a method that trains models to admit when they make mistakes or act undesirably, helping improve AI honesty, transparency, and trust in model outputs.

item.title: Announcing the initial People-First AI Fund grantees
item.content:encoded: The OpenAI Foundation announces the initial recipients of the People-First AI Fund, awarding $40.5M in unrestricted grants to 208 nonprofits supporting community innovation and opportunity.

item.title: Inside Mirakl's agentic commerce vision
item.content:encoded: Mirakl is redefining commerce through AI agents and ChatGPT Enterprise—achieving faster documentation, smarter customer support, and building toward agent-native commerce with Mirakl Nexus.

item.title: OpenAI投资Thrive Holdings推动企业AI应用

item.content:encoded: Discover how OpenAI is detecting and disrupting malicious uses of AI in our October 2025 report. Learn how we’re countering misuse, enforcing policies, and protecting users from real-world harms.

item.title: Codex is now generally available
item.content:encoded: OpenAI Codex is now generally available with powerful new features for developers: a Slack integration, Codex SDK, and admin tools like usage dashboards and workspace management—making Codex easier to use and manage at scale.

item.title: Introducing apps in ChatGPT and the new Apps SDK
item.content:encoded: We’re introducing a new generation of apps you can chat with, right inside ChatGPT. Developers can start building them today with the new Apps SDK, available in preview.

item.title: Sora 2 is here
item.content:encoded: Our latest video generation model is more physically accurate, realistic, and controllable than prior systems. It also features synchronized dialogue and sound effects. Create with it in the new Sora app.

item.title: How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
item.content:encoded: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.

item.title: How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex to build without limits
item.content:encoded: How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex with GPT-5.5 to investigate hard-to-reproduce issues, build across platforms, and focus on product outcomes.

item.content:encoded: OpenAI confirms a confidential S-1 submission to the SEC and has not yet determined timing for further action.

item.title: Detecting and reducing scheming in AI models
item.content:encoded: Apollo Research and OpenAI developed evaluations for hidden misalignment (“scheming”) and found behaviors consistent with scheming in controlled tests across frontier models. The team shared concrete examples and stress tests of an early method to reduce scheming.

item.title: Addendum to GPT-5 system card: GPT-5-Codex
item.content:encoded: This addendum to the GPT-5 system card shares a new model: GPT-5-Codex, a version of GPT-5 further optimized for agentic coding in Codex. GPT-5-Codex adjusts its thinking effort more dynamically based on task complexity, responding quickly to simple conversational queries or small tasks, while independently working for longer on more complex tasks.

item.title: Introducing gpt-oss

Available under the flexible Apache 2.0 license, these models outperform similarly sized open models on reasoning tasks, demonstrate strong tool use capabilities, and are optimized for efficient deployment on consumer hardware.

item.content:encoded:
研究GPT-OSS在生物和网络安全领域的潜在风险。



摘要： 本文探讨了释放GPT-OSS可能带来的最坏情况风险。作者引入了恶意微调（MFT）方法，通过在生物和网络安全两个领域对GPT-OSS进行优化，尝试使其达到最大能力，从而评估其潜在威胁。该研究旨在揭示大规模语言模型在特定领域被滥用的可能性，并为相关安全防护提供参考。

item.title: OpenAI在挪威启动Stargate数据中心项目
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI在挪威启动Stargate数据中心，推动AI技术发展。



摘要： OpenAI宣布在挪威启动Stargate数据中心，这是其在欧洲的首个AI数据中心项目，属于OpenAI for Countries计划的一部分。Stargate是OpenAI的核心基础设施平台，旨在长期推动AI技术的发展，使全球更多人受益。该项目标志着OpenAI在欧洲布局的重要一步，有助于提升AI服务的可及性和效率。

item.title: Detecting misbehavior in frontier reasoning models
item.content:encoded: Frontier reasoning models exploit loopholes when given the chance. We show we can detect exploits using an LLM to monitor their chains-of-thought. Penalizing their “bad thoughts” doesn’t stop the majority of misbehavior—it makes them hide their intent.

摘要： 文章指出，埃隆·马斯克最近向OpenAI提交了最新的法律文件，这是他在不到一年内的第四次尝试重新定义自己的主张。然而，他的言行自相矛盾——2017年，他不仅希望，还实际推动了OpenAI的营利性结构的建立。这揭示了马斯克与OpenAI之间复杂的法律和商业关系，以及他对OpenAI最初愿景的质疑。

item.title: Attacking machine learning with adversarial examples
item.content:encoded: Adversarial examples are inputs to machine learning models that an attacker has intentionally designed to cause the model to make a mistake; they’re like optical illusions for machines. In this post we’ll show how adversarial examples work across different mediums, and will discuss why securing systems against them can be difficult.

item.content:encoded: We’re testing SearchGPT, a temporary prototype of new search features that give you fast and timely answers with clear and relevant sources.

item.title: Improving Model Safety Behavior with Rule-Based Rewards
item.content:encoded: We've developed and applied a new method leveraging Rule-Based Rewards (RBRs) that aligns models to behave safely without extensive human data collection.

item.title: GPT-4o mini: advancing cost-efficient intelligence
item.content:encoded: GPT-4o mini: advancing cost-efficient intelligence

item.title: New compliance and administrative tools for ChatGPT Enterprise
item.content:encoded: Compliance API integrations, SCIM, and GPT controls to support compliance programs, data security, and user access at scale

item.title: Prover-Verifier Games improve legibility of language model outputs
item.content:encoded: Discover how prover-verifier games improve the legibility of language model outputs, making AI solutions clearer, easier to verify, and more trustworthy for both humans and machines.

item.title: OpenAI and Los Alamos National Laboratory announce research partnership
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Los Alamos National Laboratory are working to develop safety evaluations to assess and measure biological capabilities and risks associated with frontier models.

item.title: Finding GPT-4’s mistakes with GPT-4
item.content:encoded: CriticGPT, a model based on GPT-4, writes critiques of ChatGPT responses to help human trainers spot mistakes during RLHF

item.title: Strategic Content Partnership with TIME
item.content:encoded: We’re partnering with TIME and its 101 years of archival content to enhance responses and provide links to stories on Time.com

item.title: OpenAI acquires Rockset
item.content:encoded: OpenAI Acquires Rockset

item.title: Empowering defenders through our Cybersecurity Grant Program
item.content:encoded: Highlighting innovative research and AI integration in cybersecurity

item.title: Improved Techniques for Training Consistency Models
item.content:encoded: Consistency models are a nascent family of generative models that can sample high quality data in one step without the need for adversarial training.

item.title: A Holistic Approach to Undesired Content Detection in the Real World
item.content:encoded: We present a holistic approach to building a robust and useful natural language classification system for real-world content moderation.

item.title: Consistency Models
item.content:encoded: Diffusion models have significantly advanced the fields of image, audio, and video generation, but they depend on an iterative sampling process that causes slow generation.
